title: The Barber's
content:
Gold locks, and black locks,
        Red locks and brown,
    Topknot to love-curl
        The hair wisps down;
    Straight above the clear eyes,
        Rounded round the ears,
    Snip-snap and snick-a-snick,
        Clash the Barber's shears;
    Us, in the looking-glass,
        Footsteps in the street,
    Over, under, to and fro,
        The lean blades meet;
    Bay Rum or Bear's Grease,
        A silver groat to pay -
    Then out a-shin-shan-shining
        In the bright, blue day.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
